what_it_does: Acceptance test for the X6 ui://↔run:// context bridge. It builds a small graph, runs it to create an L6 version trail at a run:// address, then verifies that calling the internal context gather at the matching ui://canvas/<node> locus with a graph_id pulls in the run:// version history and node-instance events, while the no-graph_id path stays byte-for-byte unchanged. It also checks proximity scoring, additive behavior, graph-membership guard, end-to-end chat context, bounded cap, deduplication, fail-loud isolation, and burst-load coexistence.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN for inbox-hygiene parity with sibling suites
Creates a constant→uppercase graph and runs it twice to produce a real multi-entry L6 version trail
Calls private/internal methods such as _r2_gather, _chat_context, ref_versions, and address_tree_distance
Verifies bridged items are labelled as versions and have kind=event
Checks that bridged run:// items score at distance 0 from the ui:// locus by reusing the ui:// address for scoring
Confirms the no-graph_id gather excludes run:// strata and the bridged gather is additive
Tests a non-existent canvas node with graph_id to guard against silent wrong-node history
Exercises the production _chat_context path end-to-end with graph_id
Uses _r2_score_and_cap to verify the combined window stays within R2_BUDGET
Checks uniqueness by (kind,address,text) across both schemes
Monkey-patches suite.ref_versions to raise in order to test fail-loud isolation
Runs a 30-run burst with large outputs to verify R2_RUN_VERSIONS caps run:// contribution so a prior ui:// annotation survives
standards: The fail-loud test deliberately breaks ref_versions and asserts the gather still returns and still resolves ui:// strata
The burst-coexistence test is framed as a discriminating guard against run:// evicting ui:// memory
The docstring doubles as a design spec with explicit red-first framing and rationale for approach (b) over (a)
Bridged run:// items are scored at the ui:// locus address to avoid cross-scheme distance penalties
conventions: Follows the sibling-suite pattern of setting COMPANY_TEST_RUN
Uses a lightweight check() helper and global PASS counter
Uses a temporary FsStore and cleans up with shutil.rmtree
Names constants in uppercase for graph/node/locus addresses
Tests multiple concerns in sequential labeled parts (PART 1–6)
concerns: Relies on private/internal APIs (_r2_gather, _chat_context, ref_versions) so it is tightly coupled to implementation details
Global PASS counter is not thread-safe and mixes counting with assertion side effects
Monkey-patching suite.ref_versions may leave state if the finally block is bypassed
The 30-run burst with 160-character outputs may be slow and resource-heavy
Hardcodes node ids, graph ids, and UI locus shapes
Does not assert the exact value or default of R2_RUN_VERSIONS, only that it exists and is honored
notes: An internal acceptance test that treats the X6 bridge design doc as a test script, verifying that run:// execution memory becomes reachable from the operator's ui:// canvas-node locus without regressing the existing ui:// path.
questions: What is the exact signature and return type of _r2_gather after the X6 change?
Where is R2_RUN_VERSIONS defined and what is its default value?
How does address_tree_distance handle ui:// versus run:// addresses when both are passed?
What does _chat_context return — a string, list, or structured object?
Is ref_versions contractually guaranteed to raise on malformed or non-run:// addresses?
Are node-instance create/run events actually addressed at run://<graph>/<node> by suite.run?
What is the X8 deduplication logic and does it use exactly (kind,address,text)?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it tests the X6 bridge, preservation of the no-graph_id path, bounded window behavior, fail-loud handling, and burst coexistence as described in the docstring.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that run:// execution memory (L6 versions and node events) can be made available at the operator's live ui:// canvas-node locus by threading graph_id, while keeping the existing ui:// behavior intact and preventing run-bursts from evicting operator annotations.
novelty: The explicit cross-scheme proximity fix (scoring bridged run:// items at the ui:// locus address) and the R2_RUN_VERSIONS cap to prevent a run-burst from starving ui:// annotations, all framed as a red-first acceptance test.
contribution: A concrete acceptance test that encodes the X6 bridge contract: bridged run:// strata at a ui:// locus, additive ui:// preservation, graph-membership guard, bounded cap, dedup, fail-loud isolation, and burst-load coexistence.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the X6 ui://↔run:// context bridge, verifying that run:// version history and node events resolve at a ui:// canvas-node locus when graph_id is provided, while preserving the no-graph_id path, bounding both schemes, and surviving fail-loud and burst-load scenarios.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the behavior of the convergence bridge that merges ui:// and run:// memory into a single bounded context window without regressing existing ui:// retrieval semantics.

symbols.does: Lightweight assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts cond with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a per-check ok line. It is used throughout the test as a minimal pass/fail bookkeeping mechanism.

symbols.does: Fault-injection stub that raises ValueError to simulate an unresolvable run:// address.
symbols.description: _boom(addr, limit=25) is temporarily assigned to suite.ref_versions in the fail-loud test. It raises ValueError for any address, forcing the bridge to demonstrate that a malformed/unresolvable run:// resolution is isolated and does not crash the whole gather.
